title: Modern Operating Systems
subtitle: The Classic Textbook on the Principles and Design of Operating Systems
author: Andrew S. Tanenbaum, Herbert Bos
excerpt: The definitive OS textbook, now in its fourth edition. Tanenbaum and Bos walk the reader through the principles that underlie every modern operating system — processes, threads, memory, file systems, I/O, deadlocks, multiprocessors, and security — and ground each abstraction in detailed case studies of Linux, Windows, and Android. Comprehensive, principled, and indispensable for anyone serious about understanding how an operating system actually works.
